all_labels.explanation: States intent/progress toward producing the one-day NYC itinerary but does not itself deliver it.
Refines candidate activities for the itinerary, supporting creation of a one-day NYC plan without directly fulfilling it.
Selects low-walking transport options that shape what the one-day NYC itinerary will include.
Proposes a concrete set of NYC activities to include, materially forming the one-day itinerary content.
Finds meal options near planned stops, supporting completion of the one-day NYC itinerary.
Works on ordering and timing of the day, directly advancing the one-day itinerary structure.
General positive evaluation does not operate on or advance the specific requirement of being a one-day NYC itinerary.
Adds mobility-focused constraints that shape how the one-day NYC itinerary will be executed.
Delivers the relaxed, tailored one-day NYC itinerary, fulfilling the requirement.
Provides a specific breakfast stop as part of the one-day NYC itinerary output.
Adds a morning activity stop in NYC as part of the one-day itinerary.
Provides a specific NYC museum visit with transport guidance as an itinerary component.
Supplies a lunch stop, contributing directly to the day-plan itinerary content.
Adds an afternoon cruise activity in NYC, a direct itinerary element for the one-day plan.
Provides dinner options to complete the one-day NYC itinerary.
Specifies end-of-day return-to-hotel step, part of the one-day itinerary plan.
Adds transit constraint guidance affecting how the one-day NYC itinerary should be carried out.
Operational tip (call ahead/heart condition) supports feasibility but is not central to defining it as a one-day NYC itinerary.
Mobility-prep recommendation supports executing the day plan but only indirectly relates to the core one-day NYC itinerary requirement.
User praise/acknowledgment does not modify or add to the requirement of being a one-day NYC itinerary.
Acknowledging a new dinner preference (avoid pasta) provides rationale for switching away from the prior dinner suggestion.
Emphasizing variety motivates changing the dinner to something different than before.
Planning to review Zanmi Soho is preparatory; it supports decision-making but does not itself change dinner.
Providing a review summary for the prior option supports evaluating whether to change, but does not directly perform the change.
Listing positives of Zanmi Soho informs comparison; indirectly supports the change decision process.
Warnings about Zanmi Soho add reasons to move to a different dinner option than previously suggested.
Suggesting alternative non-pasta restaurants explicitly proposes replacing the prior dinner suggestion.
Describing an alternative option provides substance for the replacement dinner choice.
Describing Boucherie as an alternative supports selecting a new dinner, enabling the required change.
Summarizing and guiding among alternatives explicitly advances replacing the prior dinner suggestion with a different option.
Asking which option appeals solicits the decision needed to enact the dinner change.
User selects Boucherie, directly effectuating the change to a different dinner option than the prior suggestion. (Option-Select)
Assistant states the new dinner preference (Boucherie), reflecting the replacement dinner decision.
Reviewing previous context is general and not specifically about changing dinner.
Framing the audience (parents) does not address the dinner-change requirement.

Evaluating Boucherie supports validating the newly selected replacement dinner option.
Checking alignment with 'relaxed pace' is separate from the requirement to change dinner to a different option.

Additional description of Boucherie supports the new dinner selection but is not the change itself.
Final itinerary is produced with the dinner updated, fulfilling the requirement to change to a different dinner option.

Reservation note about a heart condition is not about changing dinner to a different option.

Explicitly acknowledges the requirement to avoid pasta for dinner.
Explicitly reiterates the variety/non-repeat objective that the requirement encodes.

Option-Select: explicitly states/locks in that the user's dinner choice is Boucherie, directly operating on the requirement content.
Preference-Accumulate: audience framing (parents) indirectly influences how the Boucherie dinner will be presented/fit into the itinerary.
Preference-Accumulate: accessibility/low-walking constraint affects practical suitability of keeping Boucherie in the itinerary.

Feedback-Adopt: explicitly checks that Boucherie matches the relaxed-pace need, directly validating the selected dinner requirement.

Option-Select: explicitly recommends/sets dinner at Boucherie Union Square with supporting suggestions, directly fulfilling the dinner-selection requirement.
Preference-Accumulate: booking note supports executing the Boucherie dinner plan but does not change which restaurant is selected.
Explicitly focuses planning on rainy-day backup options and nearby low-walking indoor locations—direct progress toward the requirement.
Develops indoor replacements for an outdoor stop (Washington Square Park), aligning with providing specific bad-weather alternatives.
Evaluates whether a weather-sensitive activity should be replaced; contributes to selecting indoor backups but is not itself the backup provision.
Selects concrete indoor backup options (Salmagundi Club, Fotografiska, Barnes & Noble) that satisfy 'specific' indoor alternatives.
Explicitly evaluates/endorses having a rainy-day backup plan, directly operating on the requirement’s necessity/justification.
Provides a specific indoor morning backup for an outdoor park stop, directly fulfilling part of the bad-weather backup requirement.
Provides specific indoor alternatives to a likely weather-impacted activity (boat tour), satisfying the backup-plan requirement.
Directly provides indoor rest-area options nearby (e.g., Barnes & Noble, Walker Hotel lobby), fully satisfying the 'indoor rest areas nearby' component.
Advises keeping backup options handy; reinforces usage but does not add new specific nearby indoor backups/rest areas.

Focuses on walking constraints and museum choice, not the time-span coverage.

Intent-Reveal: explicitly about sequencing events and travel times, which strongly supports covering the full day timeline.
Provides an assessment but does not evaluate or modify the morning-to-evening coverage requirement.
Walking minimization and taxi recommendation affect accessibility, not the temporal span of the itinerary.
Intent-Reveal: provides the actual one-day itinerary output, intended to run from morning through evening.
Intent-Reveal: schedules breakfast at 10:00 AM, explicitly covering the morning portion.
Intent-Reveal: adds a morning/late-morning stop after breakfast, helping fill the daytime sequence.
Intent-Reveal: provides a midday activity block, supporting continuous coverage beyond the morning.
Intent-Reveal: adds lunch, supporting mid-day coverage in a morning-to-evening plan.
Intent-Reveal: provides an afternoon activity (cruise), extending the itinerary past lunch toward evening.
Intent-Reveal: supplies dinner options, explicitly covering the evening portion.
Intent-Reveal: specifies an after-dinner wrap-up (returning to hotel), completing the evening endpoint.

Call-ahead/medical note guidance concerns safety/comfort, not itinerary time span.

Reevaluates activity options specifically through the lens of walking constraints, advancing the minimal-walking requirement.

Chooses a set of activities that are plausibly sit-heavy/low-walk, but not explicitly framed as enforcing minimal walking.
Restaurant pairing analysis can reduce walking by keeping venues near activities, indirectly supporting minimal walking.
Optimizes sequencing/travel times and adds rest periods, directly advancing the goal of minimal exertion/walking.
Positive assessment does not shape or implement the minimal-walking constraint.
Explicitly emphasizes minimizing walking and maximizing sitting, recommending taxis/rideshares as the mechanism.
Delivers the tailored itinerary in a relaxed, low-walking way, fulfilling the minimal-walking requirement in the output.
Selecting a specific breakfast stop contributes to a plan that can be arranged to minimize walking, but does not itself enforce it.
Recommends a short, sit-friendly park stop (sit/people-watch), directly implementing low-walking/rest alignment.
Provides taxi directions and a museum visit choice, explicitly minimizing walking via low-walk transport guidance.
Lunch suggestion can be compatible with minimal walking depending on proximity, but isn’t explicitly tied to the constraint here.
Adds a seated cruise activity that minimizes walking while providing sightseeing, directly fulfilling the constraint.
Dinner options contribute to the itinerary but do not explicitly ensure minimal walking without additional proximity/transport detail.
Explicitly suggests returning by short taxi, directly reducing walking at the end of the day.
Explicitly constrains transit choice (avoid subway stairs/long underground walks) to meet the minimal-walking requirement.

Explicitly sequences events, optimizes travel time, and incorporates rest periods to keep the schedule relaxed and not tiring.

Explicitly constrains the plan to minimize walking/maximize sitting and use taxis, directly targeting a non-tiring, smooth day.
Provides the actual relaxed, tailored itinerary output intended to satisfy the non-tiring/smooth schedule requirement.

Feedback-Adopt: Explicitly processes the user's breakfast feedback that establishes/anchors the 'not Caffe Reggio' constraint.
Preference-Realize: Acknowledges the explicit dislike of Caffe Reggio, directly reaffirming the requirement to avoid it.
Intent-Reveal: Reframes the task as finding alternatives, which operationalizes the constraint without yet fulfilling it.
Option-Select: Provides a concrete alternative breakfast recommendation that avoids Caffe Reggio.
Option-Select: Offers another non–Caffe Reggio breakfast option, satisfying the 'must not be Caffe Reggio' constraint.
Option-Select: Recommends Rosemary's as a replacement breakfast choice, complying with the avoidance requirement.
Option-Select: Recommends another alternative (not Caffe Reggio), meeting the constraint.
Option-Select: Asks the user to choose among alternatives, progressing toward replacing Caffe Reggio.
Option-Select: User selects Rosemary's, directly determining the replacement that ensures breakfast is not Caffe Reggio.
Feedback-Adopt: Confirms the chosen alternative, locking in the avoidance of Caffe Reggio.

Feedback-Adopt: Explicitly states that Rosemary's has been integrated, completing the replacement away from Caffe Reggio.
Preference-Accumulate: Positive evaluation supports the selection of Rosemary's but does not itself change the constraint.
Option-Select: Restates Rosemary's as the breakfast recommendation, ensuring the itinerary does not recommend Caffe Reggio.
Feedback-Adopt: Provides the finalized itinerary with the breakfast slot no longer assigned to Caffe Reggio.
Option-Select: Explicitly sets morning breakfast to Rosemary's, directly fulfilling 'must not be Caffe Reggio'.
